summary: A thorough implementation-resolved blueprint for a Projects dashboard feature covering repo management, GitHub App auth bindings, and per-project integration policy. Strong enforces/defines blocks and well-structured behavioral chains, though entity-catalog sections trade semantic density for exhaustive registry coverage.
strengths: Enforces block captures 20+ non-trivial design constraints with clear rationale — high activation preload for an implementing agent
Behavioral when/then chains model the full GitHub-first private-repo onboarding flow as a recoverable causal sequence rather than a prose narrative
Scope.*.means blocks provide inline semantic grounding that eliminates interpretive slack on key concepts like InstallationScopedAuth and RepoRootBoundary
Decision records (featureId, storage, authModel, integrationModel) resolve concrete choices with justification, correctly fulfilling the implementation_resolved class contract
usesFiles and implementsThrough sections anchor the blueprint to real package paths, supporting both agent navigation and live-use evidence
Local recoverability is high — an agent can reconstruct the full feature design from this file alone without needing the product-ideal layer
weaknesses: Entity declaration blocks (Ui with 15 entries, Api with 12 entries) function as flat registries with low semantic density per line — naming alone carries the meaning burden
Containment topology has overlap: GitHub entities appear under both Project.record.contains and Project.workspace.contains without clarifying the distinct ownership semantics
File length (~350 lines) pushes against scannability; the catalog sections could be condensed or grouped with inline annotation to improve density without losing coverage
